svg.text: An open standard by Google for seamless AI agent-to-agent communication — framework, model, and vendor agnostic
Delegate and track work via stateful
Work across any framework, model, or vendor
No custom transport, encoding, or proprietary format.
Any agent can call any agent — no custom glue code needed
N agents = N*(N-1)/2 custom connectors — brittle, impossible to maintain at scale
Lose autonomy, multi-turn conversation, async support — treated as simple functions
Each vendor has a proprietary way to connect agents — no interoperability across stacks
AgentCard advertises skills. Any client can query and invoke — no per-pair wiring needed
Multi-turn, long-running Tasks. Agents negotiate, delegate, and return Artifacts
LangChain, CrewAI, custom Python, Java — all interoperate via HTTP + JSON-RPC
FilePart — binary inline or URL ref
Discovery, delegation, multi-turn Tasks, streaming, Artifacts
Tool exposure, resource access, function calling, context injection
Together: an agent can use MCP for its internal tools, then expose its capabilities to other agents via A2A
Example: Tax Agent uses MCP to query a tax database, then responds to Law Agent's A2A Task with structured Artifacts
